title: Berlin Ticket Validation - Quick Summary
items: **Yes, you still need a valid, activated ticket.** Berlin has no barriers, but inspectors can still fine you **€60** if the ticket is missing, unstamped, or not yet activated.
The most common tourist mistake is **paper validation**: on **U-Bahn and S-Bahn** you usually stamp on the platform, while on **bus, tram, and ferry** paper tickets can be validated on board.
**Not every paper ticket behaves the same way.** Bus-driver tickets are already valid immediately, and many tickets bought from **tram machines** are already time-stamped at purchase.
On the **BVG app**, the safe rule is simple: **activate the ticket before the journey begins**. That avoids the stamping problem completely.
The other easy mistake is buying the wrong zone. **AB covers Berlin city**, while **ABC adds BER Airport, Potsdam, and Oranienburg**.
